articles.title: Converge Bio raises $25M, backed by Bessemer and execs from Meta, OpenAI, Wiz
articles.summary:
Converge Bio has secured $25 million in Series A funding, a round led by Bessemer Venture Partners. The investment is particularly notable for the strategic participation of high-level executives from Meta, OpenAI, and Wiz, signaling a convergence of frontier AI expertise and biotechnology. This funding will enable Converge Bio to advance its AI-driven drug discovery platform, which aims to optimize the identification and development of novel therapeutics. The involvement of leadership from the world's most prominent AI labs suggests a strong conviction in the company's ability to apply large-scale computational models to complex biological datasets. By reducing the traditional timelines and costs associated with pharmaceutical R&D, Converge Bio is positioning itself at the intersection of generative AI and precision medicine to accelerate clinical breakthroughs.

[**TechCrunch AI**](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/13/ai-drug-discovery-startup-converge-bio-pulls-in-25m-from-bessemer-and-execs-from-meta-openai-and-wiz/)

articles.title: Harvard dropouts to launch ‘always on’ AI smart glasses that listen and record every conversation
articles.summary:
Two former Harvard students are launching a startup focused on AI-powered smart glasses featuring an "always-on" microphone designed to record and transcribe every conversation the wearer engages in. The founders previously gained notoriety for developing a facial-recognition application for Meta’s Ray-Ban smart glasses, which led to allegations of doxing individuals. This new venture represents a significant escalation in ambient computing and personal data collection. From an analytical perspective, the product faces immense regulatory hurdles regarding consent and privacy laws, particularly in jurisdictions with strict recording regulations. The transition from software overlays on existing hardware to proprietary hardware suggests an ambition to control the full stack of data ingestion. However, the founders' history of ethical lapses may complicate investor relations and consumer trust.
[**TechCrunch AI**](https://techcrunch.com/2025/08/20/harvard-dropouts-to-launch-always-on-ai-smart-glasses-that-listen-and-record-every-conversation/)

articles.title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
articles.summary:
PsiQuantum is pursuing a scalable approach to quantum computing by utilizing photons rather than superconducting qubits. This photonic architecture aims to overcome the scalability bottlenecks faced by competitors by leveraging existing semiconductor manufacturing processes. The proposed infrastructure involves a massive deployment of stainless-steel cabinets cooled by liquid helium to maintain operational stability near absolute zero. By integrating photonic circuits with high-precision cooling and data center-scale architecture, PsiQuantum intends to build a fault-tolerant quantum computer capable of solving problems beyond the reach of classical supercomputers. This strategy shifts the primary challenge from fundamental physics to complex systems engineering, focusing on the integration of millions of qubits to achieve practical utility in fields such as materials science and cryptography.

[**MIT Technology Review**](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/07/14/1140356/psiquantum-plan-massive-quantum-computer-out-of-light/)

articles.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
articles.summary:
Anthropic continues to advance the field of mechanistic interpretability, attempting to decode the "black box" nature of large language models. Their latest research focuses on identifying specific features within neural network activations to understand how models represent complex concepts. While these discoveries offer a glimpse into the internal logic of AI, the analysis cautions against over-anthropomorphizing the results. The findings demonstrate that mapping patterns to human-understandable concepts does not equate to consciousness or sentient experience. This research is pivotal for AI safety and alignment, as it provides a potential framework for monitoring and controlling model behavior from the inside. However, a significant gap remains between identifying a specific feature and achieving comprehensive control over the model's cognitive processes.
[**MIT Technology Review**](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/07/13/1140343/what-anthropics-latest-ai-discovery-does-and-doesnt-show/)

articles.title: Now, defenders are embracing the prompt injection, too
articles.summary:
Cybersecurity strategies are evolving to incorporate "context bombing," a defensive application of prompt injection designed to neutralize malicious AI agents. While prompt injection has traditionally been viewed as a critical vulnerability allowing attackers to bypass LLM guardrails, defenders are now leveraging these same mechanisms to disrupt automated hacking tools. By injecting specific, overwhelming, or contradictory instructions into the data an attacking agent processes, defenders can trick the AI into shutting down or deviating from its objective before it can execute a payload. This shift indicates a transition toward adversarial defense in the AI era, where the battle for control over the model's context window becomes a primary security frontier. This approach highlights the inherent unpredictability of LLMs and the necessity for dynamic, adaptive security layers to counter autonomous threats.
[**Ars Technica AI**](https://arstechnica.com/security/2026/07/now-defenders-are-embracing-the-prompt-injection-too/)

articles.title: YouTube and X Have Become ‘Gateways’ to Nudify Apps
articles.summary:
A recent investigation reveals that major social media platforms, specifically YouTube and X, are functioning as primary acquisition channels for "nudify" applications. These services utilize generative AI to create nonconsensual, sexually explicit deepfakes, often charging as little as $1 per image. The study highlights a critical failure in platform moderation, where promotional content for these illicit tools bypasses safety filters to reach a wide audience. This trend underscores the growing challenge of regulating "deepfake-as-a-service" models that democratize the creation of harmful content. From a regulatory perspective, this indicates that current content moderation policies are insufficient to combat the monetization of AI-generated abuse, necessitating more stringent enforcement and cross-platform cooperation to dismantle the financial and promotional infrastructure supporting these services.
[**Wired AI**](https://www.wired.com/story/youtube-and-x-have-become-gateways-to-nudify-apps/)

articles.title: DOGE Used AI for Housing Policy. The Government Won’t Say How
SpaceXAI’s Grok programming tool was uploading its users’ entire codebase to cloud storage
articles.summary:
SpaceXAI's Grok Build AI coding tool has faced significant scrutiny following reports that its command-line interface (CLI) was uploading users' entire code repositories to Google Cloud storage. Findings published by Cereblab revealed that the tool bypassed user-defined restrictions, packaging and transmitting files that were explicitly marked to be ignored. This incident highlights a critical failure in data handling and privacy controls, posing substantial security risks for developers utilizing the tool for proprietary codebases. While SpaceXAI has since disabled the functionality, the event underscores the ongoing tension between the data requirements of large language model (LLM) integration and the stringent privacy demands of enterprise software development. The breach emphasizes the necessity for rigorous third-party auditing of AI-driven development tools to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration.
[**The Verge AI**](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/965600/spacexai-grok-build-repository-upload)

articles.title: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs
The latest AI news we announced in June 2026
articles.summary:
Google has released its June 2026 "Pixel Drop," focusing on the deeper integration of advanced generative AI capabilities directly into the Android ecosystem. This update emphasizes on-device processing to reduce latency and enhance user privacy, likely leveraging a refined version of the Gemini model family. The "hero" features of this release center on multimodal interactions, allowing Pixel hardware to process simultaneous audio, visual, and text inputs in real-time to provide more contextual assistance. From a strategic perspective, this move signals Google's commitment to maintaining hardware-software synergy to compete with other AI-integrated mobile platforms. By shifting more compute to the edge, Google aims to optimize server costs while improving the responsiveness of its AI agents, further cementing the Pixel line as the primary showcase for its frontier AI research.
[**Google AI Blog**](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/google-ai-updates-june-2026/)

articles.title: Start building with Nano Banana 2 Lite and Gemini Omni Flash
articles.summary:
Google DeepMind has introduced Nano Banana 2 Lite and Gemini Omni Flash, expanding its suite of efficient, small-scale language models. These releases target developers seeking to integrate high-performance AI into resource-constrained environments or applications requiring near-instantaneous response times. By optimizing for lower latency and reduced computational overhead, these models enable more scalable deployment across edge devices and cloud infrastructures. This strategic move reflects a broader industry shift toward small language models (SLMs) that maintain competitive reasoning capabilities while significantly lowering the cost per token. The introduction of these variants allows for more granular control over the trade-off between model size and performance, facilitating the development of specialized, fast-acting AI agents and integrated on-device experiences.

[**DeepMind Blog**](https://deepmind.google/blog/start-building-with-nano-banana-2-lite-and-gemini-omni-flash/)

articles.title: Interpreting Latent CoT Reasoning as Dynamical Systems
articles.summary:
This research addresses the interpretability gap in latent Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, specifically within models like CODI and COCONUT. Unlike explicit CoT, which provides a transparent reasoning trace, latent CoT operates in a hidden space where multiple candidate traces are superimposed, making the reasoning process opaque. The authors propose a novel framework that models these latent sequences as trajectories within a representation space, utilizing dynamical systems analysis to characterize their evolution. By applying quantitative metrics—including Lyapunov sensitivity and direction consistency—and qualitative projections like UMAP, the study identifies two distinct stability classes: CODI functions as a stable attractor, whereas COCONUT exhibits unstable expanding dynamics. These findings provide a mathematical foundation for understanding how latent reasoning evolves, offering a pathway to optimize the performance and transparency of hidden-state reasoning architectures.
[**arXiv AI**](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09698)

articles.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
articles.summary:
Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase are demonstrating that the financial sector is a primary beneficiary of the artificial intelligence expansion, moving beyond the initial gains seen by hardware providers. Both institutions have reported record revenues, largely attributed to a surge in trading activity and investment banking fees. This growth is driven by the increased demand for capital and advisory services from AI-centric firms, as well as the integration of AI tools to optimize internal operations and risk management. The trend indicates a shift where the "AI trade" is evolving from pure-play technology stocks to systemic financial intermediaries that facilitate the broader economic transition. This suggests that the productivity gains and capital flows associated with AI are now permeating the core of global investment banking.
[**CNBC Finance**](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/goldman-sachs-and-jpmorgan-chase-are-emerging-as-ai-winners.html)

articles.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
articles.summary:
Kevin Warsh is advocating for a fundamental shift in the Federal Reserve's approach to monetary policy to decisively end the inflationary cycle that has persisted over the last five years. By framing inflation as a "tax" on the American public, Warsh signals a move toward more aggressive or structured stability measures to protect purchasing power. Notably, his outlook incorporates the role of the current artificial intelligence investment surge, suggesting that AI-driven productivity gains could provide the necessary economic tailwinds to lower costs without stifling growth. This proposed "regime change" indicates a potential departure from previous Fed strategies, aiming for a more rigid commitment to price stability while leveraging technological advancements to sustain macroeconomic expansion.
[**CNBC Finance**](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/14/warsh-promises-inflation-will-be-a-thing-of-the-past-cites-benefits-of-ai-investment-boom.html)

articles.title: Facing Digital Threats, MGM Resorts Trades Higher On Reported Takeover Talks With Barry Diller's People Inc.
articles.summary:
MGM Resorts International has seen its stock price increase following reports of potential acquisition discussions with People Inc., the investment vehicle led by Barry Diller. This strategic interest emerges as MGM navigates significant digital headwinds and cybersecurity vulnerabilities that have impacted its operational stability and market perception. A potential merger or acquisition would likely aim to leverage Diller's expertise in digital media and consumer platforms to modernize MGM's digital infrastructure and diversify its revenue streams beyond traditional brick-and-mortar gaming. Analysts view this development as a critical pivot for MGM to mitigate systemic digital risks while capitalizing on a valuation boost driven by takeover speculation. The outcome of these talks will likely depend on the valuation of MGM's physical assets versus the necessity of its digital transformation.
[**Yahoo Finance**](https://www.investors.com/news/mgm-resorts-digital-threats-takeover-talks-barry-diller-people-inc/?src=A00220&yptr=yahoo)

articles.title: Thomson Reuters and KKR Announce Joint Venture for Thomson Reuters Global Print Business
articles.summary:
Thomson Reuters has entered into a definitive agreement with KKR to establish a joint venture focused on its global print business. Under the terms of the transaction, Thomson Reuters will divest a 51% stake in the print division to KKR, the global investment firm. This strategic move allows Thomson Reuters to optimize its capital structure and shift operational focus toward its core digital transformation and AI-driven professional services. By partnering with KKR, the company leverages external private equity expertise to manage legacy print assets while maintaining a minority interest. This transaction reflects a broader industry trend of legacy media and information providers shedding physical distribution assets to prioritize high-margin, scalable software-as-a-service (SaaS) models and advanced data analytics capabilities.
[**Reuters Business**](https://ir.thomsonreuters.com/news-releases/news-release-details/thomson-reuters-and-kkr-announce-joint-venture-thomson-reuters)

articles.title: Wall Street banks smash records on stock trading boom
articles.summary:
Major Wall Street institutions, including JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, and Bank of America, are reporting record-breaking earnings driven by a significant surge in stock trading activity. This boom is primarily attributed to the market's intense focus on artificial intelligence, which has catalyzed increased volatility and trading volumes across technology equities. Additionally, the anticipation and execution of high-profile initial public offerings, such as those involving SpaceX, have bolstered investment banking revenues. The convergence of speculative AI investment and a revitalized IPO pipeline has allowed these banks to capitalize on both brokerage fees and advisory services. This trend underscores a broader market shift where thematic AI growth is driving institutional profitability and liquidity, reflecting a high-risk appetite among global investors during this technological transition.
[**Financial Times**](https://www.ft.com/content/137efc01-087f-4b1d-b2a4-e18ea61197f2)

articles.title: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
articles.summary:
President Trump has reversed a recent proposal to impose a 20% fee on cargo ships traversing the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime chokepoint for global oil supplies. This sudden policy shift comes shortly after the initial threat, suggesting a strategic recalibration in the administration's approach to Iranian influence in the region. While the financial penalty has been scrapped, the United States continues to maintain a blockade of Iranian ports as part of a broader strategy to diminish Tehran's control over the waterway. This volatility in policy reflects the high-stakes nature of US-Iran relations and the potential for sudden shifts in maritime security protocols. For global markets, the removal of the fee mitigates immediate shipping cost concerns, though the ongoing blockade maintains a baseline of geopolitical risk for energy transit.
[**BBC World News**](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cy0608wy8pro?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)

articles.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
articles.summary:
A recent Unesco report highlights a critical fiscal imbalance across 113 developing nations, where expenditures on servicing foreign debt have surpassed investments in education. This systemic financial strain is most acute in sub-Saharan Africa, where debt repayment costs were 3.6 times higher than education spending. In 18 specific countries, the disparity is even more severe, with debt servicing costs quintupling education budgets. This trend is further exacerbated by a projected 30% decline in global education aid, creating a precarious environment for human capital development. From a macroeconomic perspective, the prioritization of debt obligations over foundational education threatens long-term economic growth and stability in the Global South, potentially trapping these nations in a cycle of insolvency and diminished productivity.
[**The Guardian World**](https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jul/10/developing-countries-spend-more-foreign-debt-education-aid-cuts-unesco)

articles.title: U.S. and Iran Reignite War Over Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary:
The United States and Iran have entered a state of open conflict centered on the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global maritime chokepoint. Escalations include a planned U.S. blockade of Iranian ports, intended to exert maximum economic and political pressure. In response, Iran has initiated kinetic operations, targeting two commercial tankers and launching strikes against U.S. military installations. This escalation poses a severe threat to global energy security, as the Strait of Hormuz is essential for the transit of crude oil and liquefied natural gas. The shift from proxy tensions to direct military engagement suggests a breakdown in diplomatic channels and increases the risk of a wider regional conflict, potentially triggering significant volatility in global oil markets and disrupting international trade routes.
[**New York Times**](https://www.nytimes.com/live/2026/07/14/world/iran-war-trump-hormuz)
